News.title: Millionaire CEO killed & teen son hospitalised after 30-foot launch smashed into their dinghy in hit-and-run boat crash
News.content:
DEATH ON WATER Millionaire CEO killed & teen son hospitalised after 30-foot launch smashed into their dinghy in hit-and-run boat crash

A CEO has been killed and his son hospitalised after a hit-and-run boat crash in Miami.

Davide Veglia was fatally wounded when an estimated 30ft vessel smashed into his small dinghy at around 8.11pm on Wednesday evening.





The force of the crash flung the pair into the Meloy Channel just off Miami Beach.

Two teenagers nearby recalled hearing desperate pleas for help coming from Veglia’s 14-year-old son.

“We hear ‘Help, help!’ That’s when I run to the dock, and I see like a silhouette or like a shadow of something, and we make it out and [say], ‘That’s someone, they need help, they need help’,” Samuel Londoño told WSVN.

“We instantly called the cops, let them know everything,” Enzo Avelino said.

“I yelled for him to float before the cops came, and he floated. He was, like, floating there with his dad, holding him.

“The dad was unconscious when they got him and brought him back.

Avelino added that Veglia was “foaming at the mouth” and “strapped down with an oxygen mask”.

Both Veglia and his son were rushed to Jackson Memorial Hospital, where the businessman was pronounced dead.



His son, on the other hand, only suffered a broken arm.

Police are yet to identify the operator of the multi-motor vessel which was seen cruising away moments after the collision.

It was last seen traveling north from Biscayne Bay between 7.45pm and 8.15pm.

At a press conference on Thursday, Officer George Reynaud said: “The vessel involved has not yet been identified and is described as possibly a 20- to 30-foot boat with possibly two outboard motors and is described as possibly a dark blue boat with black bottom paint.”

One local said he saw the stricken duo’s dinghy operating without any lights on.



“People need to be very careful when they are boating at night,” Larry Scheiger told Local10.

“It’s very dangerous out here, especially with a small boat.”

Pascile Padiou, who lives nearby, described the hit-and-run as “devastating”.

“You think that you are going boating with your son, it’s fun, it’s nice, and that’s a terrible accident.”

Veglia’s best friend Joao Moraes has paid tribute to the fallen executive after the tragedy.



“I can’t stand God took this man, too young. I lost my best friend,” Moraes told NBC 6.

Revealing the pair had spoken earlier that day and planned to meet on Friday, Moraes said: “I’m in shock. I sat down and cried because I lost the best person in my life.”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Veglia founded South Florida-based ABTS Convention Services back in 1995.

The company has now expanded its operations around the globe, opening offices in San Francisco, Rome and Belgrade.

News.title: ‘Kind’ woman, 45, suffocated to death after boyfriend, 49, wrapped tape around her face as man guilty of murder
News.content:
HORROR ATTACK ‘Kind’ woman, 45, suffocated to death after boyfriend, 49, wrapped tape around her face as man guilty of murder

A MAN, 49, has been found guilty of murder after suffocating a “kind” and “devoted mother”.

Norbert Maiksner brutally killed his girlfriend Frances Obiefuleh, 45, by wrapping tape around her mouth.





He launched his attack inside their home in Havant, Hampshire, on April 17 last year.

The Polish national was later found wondering on a stretch of hard shoulder on the M40 near High Wycombe in Buckinghamshire.

He told a Highways Officer he should call the police and confessed to killing his Frances.

Paul Cavin KC, prosecuting, told Portsmouth Crown Court a post-mortem revealed the much-loved mother had died of smothering with injuries caused by compression of the neck and chest.

He said the defendant also had scratches on his chest and back.

The prosecutor added: “The likely and frankly terrible conclusion is that they were inflicted by Frances with her fingers and fingernails during the attack by the defendant in her last moments as she struggled to stay alive.”

Maiksner was convicted of murder following a trial at Portsmouth Crown Court.

He was also found guilty on a child neglect charge.



The killer did not attend his trial and it went ahead in his absence.

A Hampshire Constabulary spokesperson: “During his trial the court heard that Maiksner had told officers that he had killed Frances and stated that it was all a dream and that he wanted to wake up.”

Frances’ devastated family paid an emotional tribute and said via the force: “Frances was kind, intelligent, and resourceful – a caring and deeply loving mother, sister, aunt, and friend to many.

“She was a devoted mother and beautiful in every sense of the word.

“Someone we love has been cruelly taken from us, and it has changed our lives forever.

“We have lost a truly beautiful soul.



“Her bright light has been cruelly dimmed, and we are left shaken, heartbroken, and still trying to process the grief and shock of losing Frances.”

Maiksner is due to be sentenced on March 30.

News.title: Schoolboy ‘neo-Nazi’ had high powered crossbow in bedroom & researched attack on synagogue encouraged by Russians
News.content:
A SCHOOLBOY who joined a banned far-right group and researched an attack on a synagogue, encouraged by Russian extremists, has been jailed.

The youth, who had just turned 15 at the time, planned his attacks from a remote cottage in the North of England.





Investigators discovered he had been recruited by an online bot on the Telegram social media messaging app and was being encouraged by Russian-speakers, apparently using AI to produce long daily messages in English, aimed at inspiring him to launch an attack.

Police found a series of entries from a journal in which the teenager wrote about how he wanted to launch an attack because he was being bullied at school and was hated by female pupils.

Frida Hussain KC, defending, told the sentencing hearing that the youth had been “groomed” online and his father had “reflected on the supervision he should have provided and will provide in the future.”

The judge, Mr Justice Wall, refused an application to lift the teenager’s anonymity but told him: “I hope that you now realise how seriously any involvement with terrorist organisations is treated.

“You expressed hatred for groups which are the habitual target of right-wing extremists such as the Jewish community.

“You did so in graphic and disturbing terms.

“You said at trial that you never genuinely held such beliefs. I have no doubt you were lying about that.”



Now aged 16, he appeared in court in a white collared shirt and blue jacket with a silk handkerchief in the top pocket but sobbed through much of his evidence.

He told his trial at Leeds Crown Court that he began to be bullied when he started secondary school because he was taller than everyone else and “more well-spoken than most people.”

He joined in with racist conversations on Snapchat and shared “gore” videos showing beheadings because they were “going around” and he wanted to “fit in,” he said.

When police raided the cottage, they found bomb-making manuals, chemicals to make explosives, a crossbow, five knives, two military tactical vests, military webbing, military helmets, two skull masks and a German camouflaged military jacket.

The youth’s bedroom was decorated with white supremacist flags and a Nazi SS officer’s cap and there was an Airsoft shotgun mounted on the wall onto which he had drawn far-right slogans in imitation of the Christchurch killer, Brenton Tarrant.



Michelle Heeley KC, prosecuting, told the jury that the youth had “an arsenal worthy of any young right-wing terrorist” and his diary and social media messages were the words of a teenage boy “filled with hate and racism.”

He was “building up to real life action” and had a GoPro camera in order to livestream the attack like other mass killers, she said.



The conversations moved from online chat to demonstrations of loyalty by putting up recruitment posters around a local market town, stating: “Save your race, join the Base.”

The youth also tried to recruit other users online to join the banned far-right group which encourages followers to carry out acts of violence in order to ignite a race war.

He went on to volunteer to blow up a mobile phone mast using basic homemade explosives, conducted reconnaissance and started to buy the ingredients on Amazon.

He also researched the location of the Newcastle Reform Synagogue in what prosecutors said was a rapid escalation in his behaviour.

On December 22 he used Amazon to purchase saltpetre, an ingredient of black powder explosives, and a set of kitchen scales and watched two YouTube videos on how to make the explosives.



The teenager spent Christmas Day 2024, shortly after turning 15, researching mass killers.

He was arrested at the cottage where he lived, wearing his school uniform, shortly after 7.20am on February 20 last year.

He “put his head in his hands, bent over and shook his head,” the arresting officer told the court.

He was unanimously found guilty of possession of terrorist documents, dissemination of terrorist documents and membership of a proscribed terrorist organisation and sentenced to three and a half years in prison.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Detective Chief Superintendent James Dunkerley, warned parents: “This case provides a stark reminder around the dangers of extreme content online and how individuals can be drawn into serious offending.



“All too often we see the real-world implications of individuals who have been influenced by such material or even motivated to plan or take part in terrorist activity.”

News.title: Easter holiday getaway begins as Brits battle train chaos, soaring petrol prices & airport delays as new EU checks begin
News.content:
GREAT GETAWAY Easter holiday getaway begins as Brits battle train chaos, soaring petrol prices & airport delays as new EU checks begin

AS TRAIN stations close, airports ramp up security checks and petrol prices rise holiday makers should prepare for travel chaos ahead of the bank holiday.

Those planning to get away this week for Easter half term will have to allow extra time to allow for possible cancellations or unplanned queuing.





A number of major railway stations across the UK will be closed in April, causing countless cancellations and significantly slower replacement bus services.

Both London Euston and Manchester Piccadilly station are slashing parts of their service over the next couple of weekends.



This will affect London commuters travelling north and Mancunian travellers going south.

The closure of the line between London and Milton Keynes will severely impact services to and from one of the country’s busiest stations, London Euston.

The line which connects the North with the capital will be closed while the track undergoes upgrade works alongside 270 other projects set to take place over the Easter period.

This comes as West Coast Main Line announced it will be spending £400million on investments.



Some of these lines affected run also run through Clapham Junction and London Waterloo, which will cause chaos in the capital.

Today holidaymakers who tried to escape before the weekend have been hit with unexpected delays.

A fire was reported between Sittingbourne and Faversham, meaning all lines between Ramsgate and London St Pancras, including Charing Cross, Faversham, Victoria, and Dover Priory are closed.

Disruption was expected until 4pm, but the fallout has continued into the early evening.

Sadly driving to your destination might not be the solution to your travel nightmare.



As petrol prices rise above 150p a litre for the first time in a year, motorists will have to pay eye-watering prices to travel.

Diesel drivers could be hit even worse, as prices fuel approaches 180p a litre amid the conflict in the Middle East.

RAC mobile servicing and repairs team leader Sean Kimberlin said: “Fuel prices may have risen sharply in recent weeks, but our research with drivers shows they are still determined to make the most of the Easter break.

“Despite diesel costing around £19 more per tank than last year and petrol about £8 more, only a small number of people are actually changing their plans.

“This year we’re expecting one of the busiest Easter getaways since 2022, with planned leisure trips rising again for the second year running.”



He went on to explain that traffic is likely to build from Thursday as schools break up, and the M5 towards Devon and Cornwall and M25 are expected to be particularly congested.

Kimberlin said: “The worst delays are expected from around 10am, so getting on the road early is still the best way to avoid the queues.”

For those holidaying abroad severe delays are also expected at airports, as new EU regulations security are put in place, expecting to increase passport control queues .

From April 10, all EU airports will have a new Entry/Exit system implemented.

The new system includes storing digital records of your passport, a photo of your face, and for those 12 and over, your fingerprints.



Some airports across the continent have already begun the new checks, which are set to replace passport stamps.

The system will echo the United States’ border control, as biometric details including fingerprints will begin being taken.

Minister for Border Security and Asylum, Alex Norris, said: “The EU’s Entry/Exit System will be a significant change for Brits travelling this Easter.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Always check with your transport operator in case extra time may be needed, both for your return to the UK and travelling to the EU.”

The government has advised leaving up to four hours to get through queues in the airport in accordance with this new change.

News.title: Full list of 30 postcodes across UK hit by fresh Royal Mail delays as crisis deepens
News.content:
THE Royal Mail has issued disruption alerts for 30 postcodes across the UK as crisis continues.

Homes and businesses across England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland are expected to be impacted by further postal delays.



As of today, delays are impacting as many as 30 different postcodes.



Royal Mail has attributed delays to disruptions on its air and road networks over the past 24 hours.

In a statement, the company said: “We aim to deliver to all addresses we have mail for, six days a week.

“In a small number of local offices, this may temporarily not be possible due to local issues such as high levels of sick absence, resourcing, or other local factors.

“In those cases, we will rotate deliveries to minimise the delay to individual customers.

“We’re sorry for the inconvenience and thank you for your understanding.”

What are the UK Postcodes facing delays? Birmingham East DO (B8 – B10)

Bridge of Weir DO (PA11, PA13)

Erskine DO (PA7, PA8)

Huntingdon DO (PE26-PE29)

Ilfracombe DO (EX34)

Leatherhead DO (KT21 – KT24)

Lochgelly DO (KY5)

New Ferry DO (CH32, CH62, CH63)

Newcastle Under Lyme DO (ST5, ST55)

Paisley DO (PA1 – PA3)

Prenton DO (CH43)

Quorn SPDO (LE12)

Selsey SUDO (PO20)

Sutton in Ashfield DO (NG17)

Yate DO (BS37)

Addressing the Commons, owner Daniel Kretinsky told MPs: “Of course I am deeply sorry for any letters that arrive late.”

However, he pushed back against claims that the Royal Mail’s track record for delivering letters on time was getting worse.

This is set against the backdrop of accusations workers are being asked to hide post to give the appearance delivery targets are being met.



Staff told the BBC they are often are told by managers to “take the mail for a ride” when senior management pops in for inspections.

Royal Mail customers are also facing an increase in the cost of stamps next month.

First Class stamps are set to rise to £1.80 while Second Class will now cost 91p.

News.title: Putin ‘begs oligarchs for cash’ to help boost Russia’s war-ravaged economy as cost of Ukraine invasion bleeds him dry
News.content:
CASH-strapped Vladimir Putin thrust out his begging bowl in a meeting with billionaire oligarchs as he looks to prop-up Moscow’s war-ravaged economy, according to Russian media.

The dictator reportedly held a behind-closed-doors meeting with a team of the country’s leading business magnates on Thursday.





Putin discussed military funding and Russia‘s progress in the highly-coveted eastern Donbas region of Ukraine, unnamed sources told The Bell.

Russia‘s illegal invasion of Ukraine in February 2022 has seen the country’s defence costs skyrocket, hitting 15.5trillion roubles (£144bn) in 2025.



It has left the country on the verge of an economic breakdown as the war drags on into its fifth year, as the desperate Kremlin ploughs millions into the conflict.

What’s more, Russian forces have suffered approximately 1.2million casualties and as many as 325,000 killed since February 2022.

And more than 6,000 troops were killed in a renewed offensive on the frontline between March 17 and 20, according to the commander-in-chief of Ukraine’s army.

No major power has suffered anywhere near as many casualties or fatalities in any war since World War II.



To soften the blow, Putin has turned Russia’s tycoons to provide a much-needed budget boost.

Gold mining billionaire Suleiman Kerimov pledged to 100billion roubles (£922bn) during the high-stakes meeting, according to The Bell.

While Oleg Deripaska, a metals magnate, also agreed to contribute when begged, three sources told the Financial Times.

Kremlin mouthpiece Dmitry Peskov vehemently denied claims that Putin was soliciting for cash.

But he did reveal that one businessman wanted to donate a “very large sum of money” to the state and that many present felt it was their duty to contribute after getting their start in the 1990s oligarch boom.



Referring to the faceless donor, Peskov said: “This was absolutely his initiative, and not President Putin’s.

“Although, of course, the head of state welcomed such an initiative.”

While Putin scrambles to get funds together, some predict that he could experience an economic collapse as early as next year.

Speaking on the latest episode of The Sun’s Battle Plans Exposed, Philip Ingram, a former intelligence officer, said: “We’re getting into a point of Russian exhaustion.”



Ingram predicts that Russia could face collapse by late 2026 or early 2027.

He said: “The attacks on the Russian oil and gas infrastructure, industry, generals inside Moscow, airfields, on billions of dollars worth of Russian military equipment is eating away at Russia’s continual ability to support this conflict.

“It is getting to a tipping point — and that tipping point is something that is likely to come in late 2026 or early 2027.

“Russian exhaustion is about to come upon us.



“And that will be the Russian economy suddenly realising the Russian people are in a position where they cannot continue with the suffering that they will begin to feel.”

Inflation has run rampant in Russia ever since the conflict began — even the humble cucumber has risen in price by 425 per cent, Ingram said.

Russia relies on imported fruit and vegetables, meaning prices are highly sensitive to fluctuations. In some regions, a cucumber now costs £4 — they have doubled in price since December.

Sergei Mironov, parliamentary leader of the Just Russia party said: “This winter, a new ‘delicacy’ has appeared in our shops: cucumbers.



“What are people supposed to do? Just accept that they can’t afford the most basic foods?”

RECOMMENDED STORIES

One angry shopper, Svetlana, called the prices “outrageous”.

Russian food prices have risen by 18.6 per cent in two years, according to the BBC, and as people’s economic suffering grows, Putin could lose support for his assault on Ukraine — or run out of funds altogether.

News.title: Special education teacher arrested for ‘sexually assaulting multiple students’ as she  looks teary-eyed in mugshot
News.content:
A SPECIAL education teacher has been arrested after allegedly sexually assaulting multiple students.

Nadia M. Horn posed teary-eyed for a police mugshot after she was taken into custody on Wednesday.





The 22-year-old teacher at North High School in Wisconsin was slapped in handcuffs after the school resource officer found out about her relationship with a pupil.

The Eau Claire Police Department said: “Through the course of the investigation, multiple victims have been identified.

The authorities did not specify how many victims there are exactly, nor did they give any additional details about the initial accusations.

According to district officials, the school suspended the suspect as soon as they learned of the allegations.

Read more in World News KASH GRAB FBI chief's inbox HACKED by Iran-linked cyber criminals with private pics leaked

Horn will now go without pay pending some sort of “legal resolution”.

The Police Department also learned that the suspect attended North High School.

In a letter to the concerned community, Superintendent Mike Johnson said: “Learning about alleged inappropriate conduct by a staff member is deeply troubling for us.”

“We kindly request that the school community respect the privacy of our students and their families and refrain from spreading rumors or speculation.”



Staff, including counsellors, are now on hand to support those affected throughout the week.

Horn has now been charged with sexual assault of a child by a school staff person and second-degree sexual assault of a child.

The suspect has yet to enter a plea and has not sought legal representation at this time.

The investigation is ongoing and more charges could be forthcoming, officials said.

Unfortunately, this is not the first time a teacher in the US has sexually assaulted their pupils.



Only earlier this week a teacher was arrested in New Jersey after allegedly having sex with a student.

Ashley Fisler, 36, is now facing the prospect of 20 years behind bars.

The victim told police the alleged sexual assaults took place in 2021 while they were a student at Orchard Valley Middle School.

Text messages between the pair confirmed the “unlawful sexual nature of their relationship,” officials said.

Fisler has now been charged with first-degree sexual assault of a minor.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

She has also been charged with one count of second-degree endangering the welfare of a child and one count of second-degree official misconduct.

The top charges carry a maximum of 20 years in prison, while both second-degree charges carry a maximum of 10 years.

News.title: Israel's death penalty law is 'weapon to eliminate Palestinians' claims Amnesty as 10k held
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: Israel's death penalty law is 'weapon to eliminate Palestinians' claims Amnesty as 10k held

Amnesty International told the Mirror: 'With this death penalty bill, the Israeli government is adding yet another weapon to its decades-long campaign to eliminate Palestinians. It is apartheid imposed by a noose'

View 4 Images Netanyahu's right-hand man, Ben-Gvir, has long supported the death penalty bill (Image: POOL/AFP via Getty Images)

Israel's plan to bring in a death penalty for certain Palestinian prisoners is part of its "decades-long campaign to eliminate Palestinians", a leading human rights campaign warned.

‌



It would amount to "apartheid imposed by a noose", and comes against the backdrop of an "ongoing genocide against Palestinians" in Gaza, according to Amnesty International. It also claimed Israel's authorities were torturing Palestinian prisoners "at scale".

‌



The death penalty bill faces its final vote in the Knesset next week, after it advanced on Tuesday. The legislation would be reserved specifically for those convicted of terror offences which resulted in the death of Israelis.

‌



View 4 Images Israeli military armoured vehicles (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Iran footballers take stand over US bombing of school in match ahead of World Cup READ MORE :

The bill stated: "Whoever intentionally causes the death of a person with the intention of harming a citizen or resident of Israel, with the aim of denying the existence of the State of Israel, shall be sentenced to death or life imprisonment, and one of these punishments only." The sentence would be carried out by hanging, and would be enforced within 90 days of sentencing.

The death penalty bill has been supported by one of Benjamin Netanyahu's far-right cronies; Itamar Ben-Gvir. The National Security Minister is regularly seen wearing a noose-shaped lapel pin, in a show of solidarity for the bill.

‌



Humans rights campaign group Amnesty International said the capital punishment law would be adding to Israel's arsenal in its bid to get rid of Palestinians.

View 4 Images Palestinians rush to escape the area after Israeli warplanes' bombs (Image: Anadolu via Getty Images)

Kristyan Benedict, Amnesty International UK’s Crisis Response Manager, told the Mirror: "With this death penalty bill, the Israeli government is adding yet another weapon to its decades-long campaign to eliminate Palestinians. It is apartheid imposed by a noose.

‌



"If adopted, this law will cement a system built on dehumanising policies, unchecked hate speech and institutionalised violence – the same system that is driving Israel's ongoing genocide against Palestinians in the occupied Gaza Strip and enforcing apartheid over all Palestinians.

"This is not happening in a vacuum. Israeli authorities are already torturing and killing Palestinian prisoners at scale."

Israel has reportedly started training and sorting preparations for the possible new executions ahead of the full government approval, according to Israeli media. There were reportedly about 10,000 Palestinians held in Israeli jails, as of April 2025. The bill, proposed by far-right MP Limor Son Har-Melech, would not require a unanimous verdict for judges to sentence prisoners to death.

‌



View 4 Images Unexploded munitions left by Israeli strikes may cause deadly risk to civilians (Image: Anadolu via Getty Images)

Any prisoners sentenced to the death penalty will not be able to seek a pardon. They would also be held in a separate facility with no visits from anyone, except authorised personnel.

This shocking proposal has been slammed by the EU with it's foreign affairs spokesperson, Anouar El Anouni, saying the move was "deeply concerning".

Article continues below

In a statement, El Anouni warned: "The European Union opposes capital punishment in all cases and under all circumstances. The death penalty is a violation of the right to life and cannot be executed without violation of the absolute right to be free from torture and other ill-treatment. Capital punishment also has no proven deterrent effect and renders any judicial errors irreversible."

The spokesperson added: "Israel has long upheld a de facto moratorium on both executions and capital punishment sentencing, thereby leading by example in the region despite a complex security environment. Approving this bill would represent a grave step backward from this important practice and from positions Israel has itself expressed in the past."

But, Ben-Gvir praised the execution bill earlier this week, after it passed its latest hurdle in the Knesset. He dubbed Tuesday's success as a "historic day in the State of Israel". He said: "Whoever chooses to kill Jews simply because they are Jews - their punishment is one: death. There will no longer be an open cycle of operations, imprisonment and release."

News.title: XL bully 'ate pensioner during fatal attack' before it was 'shot 10 times'
News.content:
XL bully 'ate pensioner during fatal attack' before it was 'shot 10 times'

John McColl, 84, died from his injuries a month after he was attacked by XL bully Toretti in Warrington, Cheshire, in February last year, a court heard

View 2 Images John McColl, 84, died from his injuries (Image: Cheshire Police / SWNS)

A court heard an XL bully was "eating" a pensioner, who later died.

John McColl, 84, died from his injuries a month after the attack by the dog, called Toretto, which was shot 10 times by armed police officers who were called to the scene in Warrington, Cheshire, in February last year.

Giving evidence at Liverpool Crown Court on Friday, owner Sean Garner said he took every step he possibly could to make sure the dog was safe.

He claimed the dog was locked inside a shed on the patio outside his home on Bardsley Avenue, which had a gate he said was secured by a latch and a chain, when Mr McColl wandered up the driveway at about 6pm on February 24.

View 2 Images Sean Garner denies being the owner of a dog which caused injury while dangerously out of control. (Image: ALAN DEMPSEY)

Garner said: “I could never imagine that a man who has been the pub could go up my path on his way home and do what he has obviously done to let my dog out.”

He said he was “devastated” by the incident. Garner added: “It’s horrible what’s happened to him... I’ve got no words to describe what happened to him. I wouldn’t wish it on my worst enemy.”

Garner disagreed that the dog was hungry, despite an examination finding there was no dog food in its stomach. He said: “Someone’s come into his property who he doesn’t know. It’s natural behaviour of a dog.”

David Birrell, prosecuting, said: “Your dog wasn’t guarding the premises, it was eating Mr McColl.” Garner replied: “He was locked in the shed when I went out.”

The 31-year-old, who the court heard ran a recovery business transporting cars, said he did not return to his home when police rang him about the incident because he was disqualified from driving and did not want officers to see him in a vehicle.

He agreed he was a “coward” to instead allow his pregnant partner to return to the house with their two children, which led to police arresting her. Garner said: “I was waiting to find out what had happened.”

The prosecution allege the dog was not locked inside the shed but was on the patio of the home, with only a latch on the gate preventing it from getting out.

Article continues below

Garner, of Belle Vale, Liverpool, denies being the owner of a dog which caused injury while dangerously out of control. He has previously admitted two counts of possessing an XL bully without an exemption certificate relating to Toretto and a female dog, called Malibu, found inside the house.

The trial continues.

News.title: Gaza toddler returned to family from Israel with 'cigarette burns' and 'torture' wounds
News.content:
Gaza toddler returned to family from Israel with 'cigarette burns' and 'torture' wounds

Toddler had alleged cigarette burns on his legs - the Israeli military says allegations of torture claims are “completely unfounded” and the tot's dad was a Hamas member

View 3 Images Jawad Abu Nassar was detained alongside his father (Image: Ramzi Abu Amer/APAImages/Shutterstock)

A toddler has been returned to his mother with ‘cigarette burns’ on his legs after being held by Israeli forces - with his family claiming he was ‘tortured’.

‌



Jawad Abu Nassar, aged 21 months, was detained alongside his father Osama Abu Nassar, 25, in central Gaza a week ago and returned to his family alone, 10 hours later. The Israeli military says the claims are “completely unfounded” and that Jawad’s father, who remains missing, was a Hamas member who used him as a human shield.

‌



According to the family, Osama had taken his son out at around 10am on March 19 to buy sweets ahead of the Muslim holiday of Eid al-Fitr. Osama had been grappling with severe trauma after losing his home, his unborn child, and his livelihood during the war.

‌



View 3 Images Injuries to Jawad's legs (Image: Ramzi Abu Amer/APAImages/Shutterstock)

Marco Rubio warns US's Iran war could last WEEKS as Trump begs NATO allies for help READ MORE :

His father Muhammed, told Middle East Eye: “When he left, he seemed to head east instead of west. Neighbours called me and said, ‘Hurry, your son is carrying his child on his shoulders and going east’.”

Around 200 metres from the family home in Maghazi refugee camp, Israeli forces are stationed along the so-called ‘Yellow Line’, which marks the boundary of Israeli control and a no-go zone where civilians risk being shot.

‌



Muhammed, 53, said: “The neighbours told me that (Israeli soldiers) did not shoot him, they only fired around him. Poor him - he seemed unaware of what he was doing, so he just kept walking.”

After learning his son had been detained, Muhammed went to al-Aqsa Martyrs Hospital in Deir al-Balah and left his phone number, asking to be contacted if his son and grandson were brought in.

View 3 Images Blood stained trousers (Image: Ramzi Abu Amer/APAImages/Shutterstock)

‌



Around 10 hours later, the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) called to say they had his grandson: “I rushed there with the child’s mother and found two ICRC vehicles. They handed him to us wrapped in an emergency blanket..

“I opened the blanket and saw blood stains on his trousers. I asked where the blood came from. She told me his father had a shoulder injury, and that it was his blood.”

ICRC reportedly confirmed to regional media it had received the child from Israeli authorities and reunited him with his family, but said it could not comment on his physical or psychological condition due to privacy concerns.

‌



Muhammed said: “We asked him, ‘Who caused this blood?’ He could not answer. When we asked, ‘Where is your father?’ he said only, ‘Gone.’ That was all he could say. But when his mother hugged him, he screamed and cried. She removed his clothes and found wounds on his body.”

The family claim when they took the youngster back to hospital the next day two doctors confirmed the injuries were not caused by shrapnel or other ammunition, but were consistent with signs of torture and cigarette burns.

An Israeli military spokesperson rejected the claims of abuse in a statement sent to Middle East Eye: “The claims that the IDF abused a toddler are completely unfounded and serve Hamas propaganda. On the contrary: The toddler was brought by a Hamas operative into a dangerous area to be used as a human shield.

Article continues below

“Once transferred to IDF troops, the child received care and was under the full supervision of an IDF physician and additional troops until handed over to the Red Cross at the earliest opportunity. The troops acted to ensure the toddler’s safety and provided the necessary medical attention.”

News.title: Marco Rubio warns US's Iran war could last WEEKS as Trump begs NATO allies for help
News.content:
Marco Rubio warns US's Iran war could last WEEKS as Trump begs NATO allies for help

The US war in Iran could drag on for another four weeks, Marco Rubio told allies at a G7 meeting after Donald Trump pushed back his deadline on reopening the Strait of Hormuz

View 4 Images Marco Rubio told allies at a G7 meeting that the Iran war would last another 'two to four weeks' (Image: AP)

Marco Rubio has warned NATO allies that the war in Iran could last 'four more weeks' - going beyond the original six-week deadline outlined by the White House at the start of the war.

‌



The US Secretary of State met with G7 foreign ministers, including Yvette Cooper, at a summit in France today, where the Middle East crisis dominated the agenda. During the meeting, Rubio told allies that American involvement in the conflict is likely go on for another ”two to four weeks”, reported Axios.

‌



And he admitted afterwards that the chaos in the vital Strait of Hormuz oil route is likely to pose an “immediate challenge” even after the US ends its military operations.

‌



View 4 Images US strikes on Iran began just over a month ago - and are set to continue into late spring (Image: Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images)

Petrol shortages warning as Tesco joins Asda in huge UK fuel cutback at forecourts READ MORE :

Speaking to reporters, Rubio said Iran may seek to set up a toll on all ships that use the strait, a move that he said could inflict economic pain across the world.

He said the US would seek international cooperation on a plan to keep the strait open after the current conflict ends.

‌



“Not only is this illegal, it’s unacceptable. It’s dangerous to the world,” Rubio said.

View 4 Images Donald Trump has postponed strikes on key Iranian energy targets (Image: Yuri Gripas - Pool via CNP/Shutterstock)

Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper said meanwhile warned that Iran “cannot hold the global economy hostage” after forecasts showed that UK growth could be slashed by up to half this year amid the global disruption. She added that she was “deeply concerned” about Russia’s co-operation with Iran and sought to keep up the pressure on Vladimir Putin over his invasion of Ukraine, despite the crisis in the Gulf.

‌



Ms Cooper said drones provided to Russia by Iran have been involved in strikes in Ukraine, and that there was evidence of “support from Russia provided to Iran in the Middle East conflict as well”. But echoing his boss Donald Trump, Rubio denied reports that Russia had provided Iran with information to help them target American military personnel and bases in the Middle East.

View 4 Images Iran has fired waves of missiles and drones at Israel and US allies in the region (Image: Getty Images)

“Look... let me put it to you this way. There is nothing Russia is doing for Iran that is in any way impeding or affecting our operation or the effectiveness,” he told reporters.

Article continues below

Trump meanwhile used his Truth Social platform today to further postpone his threats to bomb Iranian energy targets - claiming that talks with the regime over reopening the Strait of Hormuz were going “very well”.

During today's G7 meeting, Rubio reportedly told allies that there were two Iranian officials willing to speak with the US, but that there was ongoing confusion over who wields political power in Tehran following the death of Ayatollah Khamenei.

News.title: Liz Truss announces pro-Trump conference in UK this summer as she vows 'MAGA movement for Britain'
News.content:
America's biggest pro-Trump conference is coming to the UK, Liz Truss has announced in Texas.

The failed Prime Minister has been attending the CPAC USA conference in Grapevine, Texas this week - telling attendees she wants to start a "MAGA movement" in the UK - which she joked could be called "MEGA, Make England Great Again."

Ms Truss announced from the stage that the event would take place on 16-18 July this year in London, and tickets will go on sale this week.

Ms Truss took aim at "globalist elites", Tony Blair and former Conservative Party colleagues, saying the party she led had "lost its way".

"We need a counterrevolution to overthrow the globalist regime that are now in power," she said.

What I learned from 10 years a s govt minister trying to do things was that all the power had been handed away, handed away to unelected agencies, handed away to the climate change committee.

We did not have as Conservatives, the movement, the infrastructure, the media, the independent media to take that on.

What I'm now working on is how do we build the infrastructure. How do we build the equivalent of a a MAGA movement - a MEGA movement. Make England Great Again.

"How do we build the independent media to take on the "blob" media like the BBC. Because I know we will not get change in our country just by changing who is in No10 Downing Street. We have to change the system."

Announcing the inaugural CPAC Great Britain, she said she wanted to harness the "energy of the Conservative movement internationally.

And she said: "We want to wake up the people of Britain, who have been asleep while out country has been taken over.

Most people in Britain want to have a good job, they're proud of their country, they want it to be a Christian country. They don't want all this woke claptrap. And what we need to do is bring all those people together and create the momentum for real change."

Appearing at another event at the conference on Thursday, Truss demanded a "Trump-style revolution" in Britain in an unhinged conspiracy-theory riddled speech.

She claimed Britain had been "taken over" by Islamists and a "Global Elite."

Image: Ian Vogler / Daily Mirror) Ian Vogler / Daily Mirror)

Ms Truss branded non-right wing media in the US and UK "the enemy", saying she wanted to "cut off their oxygen".

"That's what needs to happen in America and across the western world," she told the hardcore MAGA crowd. "Please help us fight these forces in Europe, because if you don't help us defeat them in Europe, they're coming for you next."

Speaking on a main stage panel entitled "Europestan: Can Europe Survive", Ms Truss claimed "elites" in the UK "fundamentally don't like western civilisation, and they want to destroy it."

Slipping into wild, far-right conspiracy theories, she claimed a "globalist movement" had "captured the institutions, the universities, the mainstream media, the corporate sector and the bureacracy."

"They believe in open borders," she said. "They believe in human rights - by which I mean the human rights of illegal migrants - they believe in things like transgender ideology, they don't believe in the family.

"The right, and in that I include the Conservative Party in which I served as a minister and prime minister, we did not do enough to take them on."

She continued: "We went along with the status quo - things like net zero which means we have the most expensive energy in the world, we didn't reverse the changes to our constitution, we've allowed DEI to be embedded. We've allowed Islamism to grow in our country and that is the fundamental problem."

She said she had called for a "Trump-style revolution" in Britain, "because most people in our country don't believe in this stuff.

"They want to have a family, they want a nice car, they want to go on a nice holiday,they want to live in a christian country," she said.

"They don't want their country taken over."

She went on to defend Lucy Connelly, who was jailed in 2024 for inciting racial hatred after posting on X calling for a hotel holding refugees to be burned down.

"People ask me, is it really true, Liz, that people are being put in prison for talking about what's going on?" she claimed. "And I say yes it is true. We saw Lucy Connolly, a young mum, jailed for 31 months for posting on X. That is how bad it is."

She said it was a "myth" that all cultures are equal. And she claimed she had not realised the extent of the "globalist" takeover of Britain until she became Prime Minister.

"I'll hold my hands up," she said. "I did not realise how bad it was until I got into government, until I got to the top of government and I saw how it actually operated. And that has convinced me that we need to completely change the way our country is run.

"We need to change the media, we need to take on the mainstream media and we can no longer appease these forces, because things are getting worse in Britain." She warned the audience that the same thing she claimed was happening to the UK was coming to America.

"Look at what's happening with (New York Mayor Zohran) Mamdani. Look at what's happening in California. You have the same forces working in your society. We are at war with those people, we are at war for the future of Western civilisation."

News.title: FBI boss and Trump pal Kash Patel has private photos and emailed leaked by Iran hackers
News.content:
FBI boss and Trump pal Kash Patel has private photos and emailed leaked by Iran hackers

An Iranian hacking group has claimed responsibility after private photos and emails belonging to FBI director Kash Patel were leaked online, with one showing him holding a cigar to his nose

View 5 Images FBI director Kash Patel is seen with a cigar in one of the leaked photographs (Image: Twitter/X/EdKrassen)

FBI director Kash Patel has been hacked by Iran - leading dozens of his private photos and emails to be made public.

‌



A pro-Iranian hacking group claimed to have accessed an account belonging to the close Donald Trump ally and shared what appeared to be years-old photographs of him, along with a CV and other personal documents.

‌



One showed Patel posing beside an antique sports car, and another with a cigar in his mouth. The group also said that it was sharing emails and other documents from the FBI director’s account - revealing private details about his personal travels and business dealings.

‌



The message from the Iranian hacker group Handala read: "Kash Patel, the current head of the FBI, who once saw his name displayed with pride on the agency’s headquarters, will now find his name among the list of successfully hacked victims."

Petrol shortages warning as Tesco joins Asda in huge UK fuel cutback at forecourts READ MORE :

View 5 Images Another picture shows Patel posing with an antique car (Image: Twitter/X/EdKrassen)

The FBI said in a statement: “The FBI is aware of malicious actors targeting Director Patel’s personal email information, and we have taken all necessary steps to mitigate potential risks associated with this activity.

‌



“The information in question is historical in nature and involves no government information.”

View 5 Images Iranian hacker group Handala described the FBI director as a 'successfully hacked victim' (Image: Twitter/X/EdKrassen)

The Trump administration is offering a $10million (£7.4million) reward for information leading to the identification of members of the Handala hacking group.

‌



It was not clear when the hack claimed by Handala took place, though reports from December 2024 - before Patel was confirmed as director - said he had been informed by FBI that he had been targeted by an Iranian hack.

View 5 Images The Trump administration is offering a $10 million (£7.4million) reward for information on the group (Image: @alexiswilkins/Instagram)

Handala is a pro-Iranian, pro-Palestinian hacking group that earlier this month claimed credit for disrupting systems at Stryker, a Michigan-based medical technology company. Handala, which has close ties to the regime in Tehran, said the attack was in retaliation for suspected US strikes that killed Iranian schoolchildren.

‌



The Justice Department singled out Handala in an announcement last week in which it said it had seized four web domains tied to Iranian hacking schemes and threats against dissidents

View 5 Images Kash Patel become FBI director in February 2025, shortly after Trump returned to the White House (Image: Twitter/X/EdKrassen)

Describing the websites as hosting "terrorist propaganda", Patel vowed to "hunt down every actor behind these cowardly death threats and cyberattacks" and "bring the full force of American law enforcement down on them."

Article continues below

Writing on his Truth Social platform today, Donald Trump said he was "pausing" his strikes on key Iranian energy targets for another 10 days to continue with talks over ending the conflict, which he claimed were "going very well". Iran has denied that any talks are talking place.

News.title: EuroMillions winning numbers LIVE: Lottery results for March 27 with £54m jackpot
News.content:
A Brit could become an instant millionaire in tonight's EuroMillions draw.

The EuroMillions jackpot tonight stands at a staggering £54m. To win the EuroMillions, players will need to match all five main numbers and the two lucky stars. The draw is expected to take place at around 8.30pm.

The National Lottery's Thunderball will also be drawn this evening, with a top prize of £500,000. That draw is due to take place at 8pm. The window to buy tickets for both draws closed at 7.30pm.

News.title: DWP minister says 'go and check' over state pension rules changes
News.content:
DWP minister says 'go and check' over state pension rules changes

State pension payments will increase in April

View Image DWP minister Torsten Bell speaking to MPs (Image: UK Parliament)

A DWP minister has urged people to check if they will be affected by a change to the state pension. Top minister Torsten Bell urged people to check online to see if they are impacted.

The minister spoke to the Work and Pensions Committee about changes to the state pension age. The access age for the benefit is increasing from this April, going up from 66 in stages, to reach 67 by April 2028.

The state pension age is going up again from 67 to 68, between 2044 and 2046. Mr Bell spoke about what support is available for people who have to wait longer to retire and stay in work longer as a result.

He also spoke about how people can check if they are impacted by the increase in the state pension age. On this topic, he said that adverts have gone out "encouraging people to go and check their state pension age". He pointed to how people can do this online.

State Pensioners to face major tax change

The minister said: "There are digital tools that enable people to know their state pension age. All people need to do is put their date of birth into the Work out your State Pension age tool and it tells them straight away." There is a tool to do this on the Government website.

You can use this tool to check your state pension age as well as your bus pass age, when you will qualify for free bus travel. Mr Bell cited a survey that indicated that 96 per cent of people aged 60 to 64 say it is very or fairly easy to find out their state pension age.

The minister said: "That is good news. Our wider work is to drive people to those tools. Some of that is around radio campaigns—you will probably hear some of those—where the evidence shows it is most likely to have an effect. They have higher trust in radio advertising than others."

Letters going out

He said that the previous Conservative Government had written to everyone impacted by the gradual move from 66 to 67, back in 2016. He said the DWP under the current Government plans to continue to write to people approaching retirement age.

Mr Bell said: "Three million letters have already gone to people who will be in the 67 cohort—the ones that are coming after 2028. I am sure we need to keep doing more on that front."

The minister said the DWP is also working "closely" with employers to make sure they are aware of the changing state pension age. He explained: "We work with HMRC, who have bulletins that go directly to employers.

"It is important that it is not just about individuals knowing about the state pension age.

Article continues below

"Part of this is about employers expecting workers to either need or want to work later, so the communication is also with them."

News.title: Why mountainous coastline gives Iran such control over Strait of Hormuz
News.content:
Around 20% of the world’s oil supply normally passes through the Strait of Hormuz - the narrow waterway between Iran and the horn-like tip of the Arabian peninsula.

Right now it is effectively closed, with Iran deciding which ships can get through, and the impact is being felt worldwide.

The BBC's Diplomatic Correspondent Paul Adams explains why navigating this key shipping channel could be so dangerous.
